title: Lead-to-Revenue Funnel Conversion Rates
dataSources: Primary spine: Splunk Add-on for Salesforce (Splunkbase 3549) ingesting Lead API fields into `index=business` `sourcetype="sfdc:lead"` via REST snapshot or Bulk API 2.0 job results (`POST /services/data/v60.0/jobs/query` with SOQL selecting `Id`, `Status`, `LeadSource`, `IsConverted`, `ConvertedDate`, `ConvertedOpportunityId`, `Rating`, `LastModifiedDate`, plus optional custom roll-up fields your RevOps maps for closed-won dollars, e.g. `OppClosedAmt__c` denormalized from Opportunity for funnel tables). Parallel warehouse path: Splunk DB Connect (Splunkbase 2686) `db_input` JDBC to `crm.lead_fact` aligned to the same grain, `sourcetype="dbx:crm_leads"`, carrying `mktoStatus` or `lifecyclestage` from Marketo (`https://{munchkin_id}.mktorest.com/rest`) or HubSpot (`GET https://api.hubapi.com/crm/v3/objects/contacts?properties=lifecyclestage,hs_lead_status`) replication jobs. Heavy Forwarder modular inputs poll Marketo `/rest/v1/leads.json`, Pardot `/api/v5/objects/prospects`, or Eloqua contact exports into `marketo:leads`, `pardot:prospect`, `eloqua:contact` when CRM does not carry MAP-native lifecycle labels — normalize onto shared staging sourcetypes before unioning into business dashboards.
app: Splunk Add-on for Salesforce ([Splunkbase 3549](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/3549)), Splunk DB Connect ([Splunkbase 2686](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2686)), Custom modular inputs or Splunk Add-on Builder REST pollers for Adobe Marketo Engage, HubSpot (`OAuth2` / Private App token), Salesforce Pardot API v5, Oracle Eloqua REST — optional Splunk HTTP Event Collector on the Search Head or Heavy Forwarder for HubSpot webhook payloads (`contact.lifecycleStageChange`). Package names commonly include `Splunk_TA_salesforce` and `splunk_app_db_connect`.
spl:
index=business sourcetype="sfdc:lead" earliest=-90d
| eval status_norm=lower(coalesce(Status, mktoStatus, lifecyclestage))
| eval lead_source=coalesce(LeadSource, source)
| eval win_amt=tonumber(coalesce(OppClosedAmt__c, ClosedWonAmount__c, Amount, "0"))
| eval is_mql=if(match(status_norm,"qualified|mql|marketing.qualified|marketingqualifiedlead"), 1, 0)
| eval is_sql=if(match(status_norm,"sql|sales.qualified|sales.accepted|salesqualifiedlead|sales.accepted.lead"), 1, 0)
| eval is_opportunity=if(isnotnull(ConvertedOpportunityId) OR match(status_norm,"opportunity"), 1, 0)
| eval is_won=if(match(status_norm,"closed.won|customer|won"), 1, 0)
| stats dc(Id) as total_leads,
        sum(is_mql) as mqls,
        sum(is_sql) as sqls,
        sum(is_opportunity) as opportunities,
        sum(is_won) as closed_won,
        sum(eval(if(is_won=1, win_amt, 0))) as revenue
   by lead_source
| eval lead_to_mql_pct=if(total_leads>0, round(100*mqls/total_leads, 1), null())
| eval mql_to_sql_pct=if(mqls>0, round(100*sqls/mqls, 1), null())
| eval sql_to_opp_pct=if(sqls>0, round(100*opportunities/sqls, 1), null())
| eval opp_to_won_pct=if(opportunities>0, round(100*closed_won/opportunities, 1), null())
| eval overall_pct=if(total_leads>0, round(100*closed_won/total_leads, 2), null())
| eval avg_deal_size=if(closed_won>0, round(revenue/closed_won, 0), null())
| sort - total_leads
| table lead_source, total_leads, lead_to_mql_pct, mqls, mql_to_sql_pct, sqls, sql_to_opp_pct, opportunities, opp_to_won_pct, closed_won, overall_pct, revenue, avg_deal_size
description: Measures stage-by-stage conversion — visitor-attributed leads through marketing-qualified, sales-qualified, opportunity creation, and closed-won — rolled up per CRM lead source so RevOps can see where volume collapses versus pipeline-generation narratives alone. It deliberately scores funnel leakage using normalized lifecycle signals drawn from Salesforce Lead rows enriched or warehouse-joined with Marketo `mktoStatus`, HubSpot `lifecyclestage`, or Pardot `lifecycle_stage`, not campaign cash-on-cash ROI (UC-23.3.1) and not raw web session share by channel (UC-23.3.4). The search produces B2B benchmark-friendly ratios (visitor→lead assumptions sit upstream in MAP ingestion) matching SiriusDecisions / Forrester Demand Waterfall stage semantics when your governance tables align SAL versus SQL.
value: Marketing ops weekly reviews and Sales–Marketing SLA scorecards need one Splunk-native picture of drop-off between MQL and SQL acceptance and between SQL and opportunity creation — where alignment meetings spend ninety percent of airtime — plus pipeline coverage commentary for CMO board pre-reads when leadership asks whether weak bookings trace to demand quality versus sales capacity. Surfacing territory-level imbalance separately from MAP-driven scoring drift separates policy knob turns (threshold moves from fifty to seventy-five points) from genuine leakage that warrants rerouting budget or fixing assignment logic.
implementation: Land `sfdc:lead` via Splunkbase 3549 inputs or Bulk API modular ingest with watermark on `LastModifiedDate`; optionally enrich Lead rows through DB Connect snapshots from your CRM warehouse where MAP lifecycle columns already align to Demand Waterfall naming; publish the SPL as `rpt_lead_to_revenue_by_source`, clone macros that encapsulate your governance-driven `case()` mapping from vendor-native statuses into SAL or SQL buckets, wire Dashboard Studio funnel and heatmap panels to the same saved search output, schedule week-over-week delta tiles for MoM cohort views owned by Revenue Marketing.
visualization: Dashboard Studio (or Classic): splunk.funnel for ordered stage counts (Lead → MQL → SQL → Opportunity → Closed Won) with inline stage-to-stage percentage labels; horizontal splunk.bar of overall_pct or lead_to_mql_pct by lead_source; splunk.singlevalue tiles for each headline ratio with seven-day sparkline and week-over-week arrow token; cohort-style splunk.table of lead-creation month × maximum stage reached from a companion search; splunk.choropleth or matrix heatmap sourcetype panel when you add a split field (region × stage) using the same rollup with an added by-clause.

references.title: Salesforce REST API Developer Guide — sObject basics
HubSpot CRM — Contacts API (lifecycle and lead status)
Adobe Marketo Engage REST API overview
Forrester — B2B marketing and the demand waterfall (methodology context)

knownFalsePositives:
- **MAP Scoring-Model Refresh**: Marketo / Eloqua / Pardot scoring-model rebuild compresses everyone below the 'new MQL' bar until nurture catches up. Suppress via the change-window register `marketing_automation_scoring_register.csv` keyed on `scoring_model_id`+`schema_version`+CHG_id (TTL <= 30 days post-release) and a `where NOT scoring_model_change_active` filter.
- **Webinar / Event-Burst Inflation**: a single large webinar floods early-stage MQLs while downstream acceptance queues stay flat, resembling leakage. Suppress via the change-window register `marketing_event_register.csv` keyed on `event_id`+`webinar_platform` (TTL <= 21 days from `event_date`) and a `where NOT event_burst_active` filter; require Field-Marketing Lead sign-off.
- **Data-Cloud Sync Lag**: Salesforce Data Cloud / HubSpot CRM extract lag breaks the join between MQL and SAL/SQL stages. Suppress via the lookup `crm_extract_sync_lag_register.csv` keyed on `crm_source`+`extract_window_id` (TTL <= 6 h) and a `where crm_sync_complete=true` filter; require warehouse watermark audit before alerting.
- **Pipeline Hygiene Sweeps**: bulk close-lost on stale opps inflates leakage indicators. Suppress via the change-window register `crm_pipeline_hygiene_register.csv` keyed on `crm_change_id`+`scope` (TTL <= 7 days post-sweep) and a `where NOT pipeline_hygiene_active` filter.
- **Field Mapping / Lifecycle-Stage Renames**: HubSpot lifecycle-stage renames or Salesforce field-mapping changes invalidate macros. Suppress via the lookup `lifecycle_stage_taxonomy_register.csv` keyed on `field_name`+`field_version` and a `where lifecycle_taxonomy_aligned=true` filter; freeze taxonomy edits monthly with ServiceNow CHG attestation.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
Splunk Enterprise or Cloud roles granting `index=business` search plus administrative rights for Splunk DB Connect (Splunkbase 2686) JDBC identities and Splunk Add-on for Salesforce (Splunkbase 3549) credential objects stored encrypted via Storage Passwords. Salesforce integration profile needs API Enabled plus field-level read on `Lead.Status`, `Lead.LeadSource`, `Lead.ConvertedOpportunityId`, `Lead.ConvertedDate`, `Lead.LastModifiedDate`, and any custom currency carry-forward fields RevOps exposes for funnel dashboards — hide `Amount` on Lead unless intentionally replicated from Opportunity through Apex or nightly jobs. Network egress from Heavy Forwarders to `login.salesforce.com`, HubSpot `api.hubapi.com`, Marketo-host `*.mktorest.com`, Pardot `pi.pardot.com`, Eloqua REST hosts per tenant region; webhook ingress only where InfoSec approves inbound HEC for HubSpot staging subscriptions.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Install Splunk Add-on for Salesforce on Forwarders or Search Heads per vendor guidance; configure Bulk API 2.0 input or Rising Column REST pulls targeting Lead objects with incremental predicate `LastModifiedDate > watermark`. Emit sourcetype `sfdc:lead` into `business`. Where CRM lacks MAP lifecycle mirrors, schedule Splunk DB Connect JDBC pulls against Snowflake or BigQuery warehouse tables populated by marketing automation CDC streams — emit `dbx:crm_leads` with lowercase mirrors (`lifecyclestage`, `mkto_status`) unified downstream via macros. Optional Heavy Forwarder modular Python scripts authenticate HubSpot Private Apps (`Bearer`) or Marketo OAuth (`POST https://{id}.mktorest.com/identity/oauth/token`) before paging REST endpoints (Marketo `/rest/v1/leads.json`, HubSpot `/crm/v3/objects/contacts`). Rate-limit polls (HubSpot burst tiers ~100 requests per ten seconds); stagger MAP polls five minutes apart from Salesforce TA pulls so Splunk `_time` spikes do not collide during burst closes.

Step 2 — Encode funnel governance before alerts
Persist `macros.conf` entries such as `normalize_stage(status_norm)` translating Salesforce “Working - Contacted”, Marketo “Sales Accepted Lead”, HubSpot `salesqualifiedlead`, and Pardot acceptance booleans into canonical SAL versus SQL booleans rather than relying solely on permissive regex inside alerts — regex assists onboarding dashboards while governance macros remain authoritative when SiriusDecisions semantics conflict with literal vendor strings. Wire HubSpot webhooks (`contact.lifecycleStageChange`) through HEC into `hubspot:lifecycle` when near-real-time funnel telemetry beats nightly warehouse snapshots — normalize webhook payloads through props/transforms into `lifecyclestage` alongside TA-fed CRM rows.

Step 3 — Publish the Splunk correlation saved as `rpt_ltv_by_lead_source`
Paste the field-standardized SPL from the top-level `spl` element — opening predicate restricts ninety-day rolling Lead snapshots used by Marketing Ops cadences; `status_norm` collapses heterogeneous MAP vendor spellings when warehouse replication landed auxiliary columns beside Salesforce-native `Status`; `win_amt` prefers RevOps-maintained roll-ups (`OppClosedAmt__c`) because native Lead payloads omit Opportunity Amount until custom enrichment lands — absence yields zero-dollar wins triggering governance alarms rather than silent null math; trailing `stats` aggregates distinct Salesforce Lead IDs per lead_source because recycled contacts otherwise distort funnel denominators when duplicate IDs survive nurture reopen flows; downstream ratio fields guard divide-by-zero after sparse Partner-sourced cohorts.

Step 4 — Validate against Salesforce Lightning before executive circulation
Verify against the Salesforce dashboard that Lead rows exported from Splunk match Status and Lead Source by sampling Lead IDs side by side before the CMO pack circulates — reconcile Bulk API replication lag (typically minutes when TA incremental queries succeed). Cross-check aggregate closed-won tallies against Opportunity-only Salesforce dashboards filtered `StageName = Closed Won` joined through `ConvertedOpportunityId` where Lead-derived flags disagree — reconcile mismatches using Opportunity-centric sibling UC-23.2.1 exports rather than trusting regex-only Lead rows alone. Confirm MAP vendor consoles (Marketo Revenue Cycle Analytics or HubSpot funnel reports) agree directionally on stage populations though timestamps differ — Splunk `_time` ties to ingestion stamps while MAP UI ties to behavioral timestamps — document ±twenty-four-hour reconciliation tolerance inside runbook footnotes.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
Missing fields: NULL `ConvertedOpportunityId` on rows you expect won — integration user field-level security or SOQL omissions usually cause empty values; tail `splunkd.log` for API connection errors (HTTP 401/403) from the TA against `login.salesforce.com` before blaming funnel math.
Bulk API job polling failures returning HTTP 429 `REQUEST_LIMIT_EXCEEDED` JSON bodies — widen spacing between incremental pulls.
Lead-status normalization drift — Salesforce picklists labeled “Working - Contacted” diverge from Marketo “Sales Accepted Lead” and HubSpot `salesqualifiedlead`; maintain CSV lookup `_normalize_stage.csv` keyed by concat(system,vendor_status) feeding macros instead of enlarging brittle regex fragments whenever Sales Ops renames stages.
Field-update lag — Marketo Salesforce sync defaults five-minute cadence yet bursts thirty-plus minutes behind during Dreamforce-scale uploads; nightly warehouse snapshots flatten intra-day transitions — Splunk ratios lag MAP transient spikes unless Rising Column TA pulls shorten latency.
Lead conversion mechanics — `Lead.IsConverted=true` freezes Lead rows while Opportunity becomes pricing authority — funnel searches counting wins solely from Lead regex miss Closed Won booked solely on Opportunity — blend Opportunity joins keyed `ConvertedOpportunityId = Opportunity.Id` when Revenue Recognition insists CRM Opportunity amounts supersede Lead duplicates.
Recycled leads — Marketo transitions Disqualified prospects back through Recycled states causing duplicate funnel traversals unless Splunk dedupes via stable person UUID (`lead_partition_marketo_guid`) paired with email hash.
MQL threshold drift — raising MAP numeric cutoff mid-quarter slashes counted MQLs thirty-to-fifty percent independent of inbound volume — annotate dashboards when governance publishes threshold edits.
Sales-acceptance SLA masking — twenty-four-hour accepted-MQL versus seventy-two-hour auto-disqualify windows encode operational backlog — apparent SQL slump sometimes reflects tardy response scoring rather than upstream MAP hygiene.
Round-robin imbalance — uneven assignment quotas mimic territory conversion deltas despite homogeneous inbound quality — overlay routing-queue fairness charts sourced from Salesforce Assignment Rules logs.
Opportunity creation lag — MAP automation stamping Opportunities when SAL arrives contrasts reps delaying Opp creation until BANT confirmation — SQL→Opportunity ratios oscillate based on operational discipline unrelated to Splunk ingestion integrity.
Lead-source mis-attribution — Apple Mail Privacy Protection and stripped UTMs inflate LeadSource=`Direct` share — downstream conversion comparisons skew toward bogus organic/direct excellence unless MAP first-touch IDs augment Splunk joins.
ABM pathways — Named-account plays opening Opportunities without traversing lead funnel stages collapse synthetic conversion percentages unless filtered ABM cohorts exclude MAP-centric denominators.
Multi-product distortion — shared Accounts linking Product-A nurture leads with Product-B Opportunities inflate conversion math unless Splunk splits `Lead.RecordTypeId` or product-interest dimensions.
Closed-Lost reactivation — reopened nurture journeys yield non-monotonic histories — timeline cohort searches must anchor first-touch cohort month separately from latest stage snapshot.
Forecast versus booked revenue lag — Closed Won Amount closes weeks ahead of Finance recognition — Splunk pipeline ratios reconcile CRM snapshots faster than ERP revenue recognition accepted by CFO packs.
Pardot B2BMA gaps — Letter grades (`A–F`) remain inside B2B Marketing Analytics datasets absent incremental Salesforce fields unless Insights connectors synchronize.
HubSpot stage-skipping — Lifecycle automation jumps straight from lead to opportunity — enforce supplemental Opportunity-created searches capturing skips Splunk funnel tiles otherwise omit.
Routing contention — Parallel Marketo Dynamic Chat routing versus Salesforce Omni queue reassignment swaps OwnerId mid-flight — SQL counts hinge on auditing whichever system owns authoritative acceptance timestamps Splunk macros reference.
grandmaExplanation: We watch how many interested people move step by step from first raising their hand through marketing vetting, sales vetting, a real deal, and finally a signed win — broken out by where they came from — so we see where the pinch is instead of arguing from gut feelings.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Inject 90 days of synthetic Marketo lead events plus Salesforce opportunity records for 25,000 leads / $48M pipeline. Days 1-45 use baseline conversion: lead->MQL=22%, MQL->SAL=58%, SAL->SQL=64%, SQL->closed-won=24%. Days 46-90 force MQL->SAL to drop to 19% (a -39% relative drop) while lead volume stays flat. Confirm the saved search `lead_to_revenue_funnel_drop_v3` fires with `severity=high`, `notable_index=notable`, attaches `bottleneck_stage=mql_to_sal`, `pipeline_at_risk=$8.6M`, drives a SOAR playbook (`l2r_remediation_v2`) creating a Salesforce CRM case, and notifies VP-Sales via PagerDuty integration.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Pre-populate `marketing_automation_scoring_register.csv` with `scoring_model_id=MQL_V3_2025`, `schema_version=v3`, `change_start=now()-7d`, `change_end=now()+23d`, `change_ticket_id=CHG0019712`, `approver=marketing_ops_lead@example.com`, `expected_mql_drop_pct=35`. Replay 30 days of synthetic events with MQL counts dropping 35% during the registered window. Confirm `lead_to_revenue_funnel_drop_v3` does NOT fire (eval `scoring_model_change_active=1` excludes the cohort from MQL aggregations). Confirm the audit dashboard `L2RFunnelExceptions` records the suppression with reason=`scoring_model_refresh` and SHA-256 hash.

evidence: Dashboard Studio panel `LeadToRevenueExec` archived weekly to `evidence_index` with `evidence_type=lead_revenue_funnel_health` and a SHA-256 hash. Saved-search run history exported via `| rest /services/saved/searches/lead_to_revenue_funnel_drop_v3` with `latest_runtime_iso` and result fingerprints. Marketo / Salesforce HMAC-SHA256 webhook signature-verification logs cross-referenced with funnel events. ServiceNow CHG records linked from change-window registers; quarterly RevOps sign-off retained 13 months in `index=audit`.
exclusions: ABM (Account-Based Marketing) target-account engagement scoring (Demandbase / 6sense) excluded — handled in UC-23.3.6. Field-events booth-scan engagement out of scope (covered by UC-23.3.7). Customer-marketing post-sale expansion / cross-sell tracked in UC-23.5.6. Subscription renewal scoring (Gainsight) excluded — measured in UC-23.1.7.
